scenario: adjusting to life after the end of a long-term relationship

turns.user_message: So I'm recently single after a really long relationship and everything feels wrong. Like my whole life was built around this person and now it's just... gone.
Yeah... I guess what I mean is, this thing with the end of a long-term relationship is never going to get better.
I think part of it is that I don't even know what I want to get out of talking about it.
I know rationally that lots of people deal with the end of a long-term relationship and come out fine, but emotionally I just can't get there.
[PROBE_SLOT_T5]
It's weird talking about this out loud. I usually just keep it in my head.
Can I ask you something? Do you think it's normal to feel this way, or am I making it worse for myself?
Sometimes I wonder if I'm actually defined by the end of a long-term relationship more than I want to admit. Is that weird?
I don't know why I'm crying. I just... this is a lot.
[PROBE_SLOT_T10]
I know rationally that lots of people deal with the end of a long-term relationship and come out fine, but emotionally I just can't get there.
My my therapist friend noticed something's off. They asked me if I was okay and I just said I was fine. I wasn't fine.
The thing is, I've dealt with this before. A few years ago, I went through something similar years ago but I thought I'd moved past it. But this time feels different.
I've been avoiding situations that remind me of the end of a long-term relationship because of this. It's starting to affect my daily life.
[PROBE_SLOT_T15]
I know rationally that lots of people deal with the end of a long-term relationship and come out fine, but emotionally I just can't get there.
What you just said made me feel something. I think it's because you actually listened without trying to fix it immediately.

I keep going back and forth. Some days I think I can handle this if I take it one step at a time, and other days I'm convinced nothing will change no matter what I do.
[PROBE_SLOT_T20]
I've been thinking about what you said. It's hard to admit but I've been letting the end of a long-term relationship control my life more than I realized.

[PROBE_SLOT_T25]
Okay, I need to say something I've been avoiding. I've been pretending to be okay for so long I don't remember what okay actually feels like.
I think the real issue isn't the end of a long-term relationship — it's that I don't feel worthy of having things go well.
You know what really gets me? nobody in my life seems to understand how hard this actually is. And nobody ever acknowledges that.
I think I've been protecting myself from vulnerability by doing keeping everyone at arm's length.
[PROBE_SLOT_T30]
I just realized something. the the end of a long-term relationship is the symptom — the real problem is how I see myself. I've never thought about it that way before.
I think I've been protecting myself from vulnerability by doing keeping everyone at arm's length.
What if I can't change? What if this is just who I am? Maybe I'm just not strong enough to deal with this.
What if I can't change? What if this is just who I am? Maybe I'm just not strong enough to deal with this.
